questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a complete stop and wait until the light turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light require you to do?
choices: Stop completely, then proceed when safe
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is out of service
Make a full stop and yield to the left
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light warns drivers to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What is the shape of a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red eight-sided octagon, a shape used for no other sign so it is recognizable even from behind.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal mean?
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow while protected from conflicting traffic
Oncoming traffic also has a green light
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected movement in the direction it points, meaning conflicting traffic is stopped.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
The light will turn green next
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming; stop if you can do so safely rather than racing through.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the center line mean?
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because oncoming traffic cannot be seen safely.

questions.prompt: What color and shape is a typical warning sign that alerts you to conditions ahead?
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn drivers of hazards or changing conditions ahead, such as curves or merges.

questions.prompt: What does a triangular sign pointing downward mean?
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power failure, you should treat the intersection as if it has what?
choices: A green light in all directions
A yield sign only for left turns
questions.explanation: A dark or non-working signal should be treated as an all-way stop, with each driver stopping and proceeding in turn.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must yield to whom?
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians crossing your path
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A left turn requires you to yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians before crossing their path.

questions.prompt: In New Jersey, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a full stop, when the way is clear and no sign prohibits it
Only when an arrow is shown
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed after a complete stop when traffic and pedestrians are clear, unless a sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a marked crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to and stop for pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When you approach a vehicle already traveling in a traffic circle or roundabout, you should do what?
choices: Yield to the vehicle already in the circle
Expect the other vehicle to yield to you
questions.explanation: Vehicles already in a traffic circle or roundabout generally have the right-of-way over those entering.

questions.prompt: What must you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with flashing lights and a siren?
choices: Pull to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the area
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Before turning, how do you let others know your intentions?
choices: Signal at least 100 feet before the turn
Turn without any signal if no one is near
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet before a turn warns other drivers and pedestrians of your intentions.

questions.prompt: When you are entering a roadway from a private driveway, you must do what?
choices: Yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Stop only if a car is within 10 feet
questions.explanation: A driver entering a road from a driveway or alley must yield to all traffic and pedestrians already there.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, which vehicle generally yields?
choices: The vehicle on the left yields to the vehicle on the right
The vehicle on the right yields to the left
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, the driver on the left must yield to the driver approaching from the right.

questions.prompt: From which lane should you generally begin and end a left turn on a two-way street?
choices: From the lane closest to the center line into the nearest left lane
questions.explanation: Begin a left turn from the lane nearest the center line and finish in the lane just to the right of the center line.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the speed limit in a New Jersey residential or business district?
questions.explanation: New Jersey's statutory speed limit in business and residential districts is 25 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default New Jersey speed limit on most roadways that are not business, residential, or designated highways?
questions.explanation: New Jersey law sets a default limit of 50 mph on most other roadways where no higher limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What is a safe minimum following distance behind the vehicle ahead under good conditions?
choices: At least three to four seconds
Ten car lengths regardless of speed
questions.explanation: A following distance of at least three to four seconds gives you time to react and stop safely.

questions.prompt: Unless posted otherwise, what is the speed limit in a New Jersey school zone when children are present?
questions.explanation: The statutory speed limit in a school zone is 25 mph when children are present or going to and from school.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect your vehicle's stopping distance?
choices: Stopping distance increases sharply as speed increases
Speed has no effect on braking
questions.explanation: Stopping distance grows much faster than speed, so doubling your speed more than doubles the distance needed to stop.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance?
choices: In rain, snow, fog, or heavy traffic
Never; three seconds is always enough
questions.explanation: Poor weather and heavy traffic reduce traction and visibility, so a longer following distance is needed.

questions.prompt: Even when traveling at the posted limit, you must still do what?
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable and safe for conditions
Maintain the maximum speed at all times
Match the fastest car around you
questions.explanation: You must always drive at a safe speed for current conditions, which may be slower than the posted limit.

questions.prompt: What should you do when another driver is following you too closely (tailgating)?
choices: Slow down gradually and let them pass
Speed up well over the limit
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and letting a tailgater pass increases the space ahead of you and reduces the risk of a rear-end crash.

questions.prompt: Unless posted otherwise, what is the speed limit in certain New Jersey suburban business and residential districts?
questions.explanation: New Jersey sets a 35 mph statutory limit in certain suburban business and residential districts unless signs differ.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to handle a sharp curve?
choices: Slow down before entering the curve
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve lets you keep control, since braking hard in a curve can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: When is it legal to pass another vehicle using a broken yellow center line?
choices: When the lane ahead is clear and it is safe to do so
Only when the other driver waves you on
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line means passing is permitted when the oncoming lane is clear and it is safe.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, which lane should you generally use except when passing?
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass so faster traffic can use the left lane to overtake.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what should you always do in addition to signaling?
choices: Check mirrors and look over your shoulder for blind spots
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show blind spots, so a shoulder check is needed before moving over to confirm the lane is clear.

questions.prompt: Where is passing generally prohibited?
choices: On hills and curves where you cannot see oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: You must not pass where your view is blocked, such as hills and curves, because oncoming traffic may be hidden.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when should you return to the right lane?
choices: Once you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Immediately, even if you are beside it
questions.explanation: Return to the right lane only after you can see the whole passed vehicle in your mirror, leaving it safe space.

questions.prompt: When may you legally pass on the right?
choices: When there are two or more lanes traveling in your direction
Whenever the car ahead is slow
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is only allowed where the road has two or more lanes going your way.

questions.prompt: What should you do if another driver is passing you?
choices: Maintain your speed or slow down slightly
Speed up to prevent the pass
questions.explanation: Keeping your speed steady or slowing slightly lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: You should never pass within how far of an intersection or railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: Passing within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing is prohibited because of crossing traffic.

questions.prompt: When completing a pass, you should never exceed what?
questions.explanation: You must complete a pass without going over the speed limit, even if it takes longer to do so safely.

questions.prompt: What does a solid white line between lanes traveling the same direction indicate?
choices: Lane changing is discouraged or prohibited
questions.explanation: A solid white line marks lanes going the same direction and discourages or prohibits crossing it to change lanes.

questions.prompt: What are the large blind spots around a tractor-trailer called?
questions.explanation: A truck's blind spots are called no-zones; if you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: Under New Jersey's Move Over law, what must you do when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Move over a lane if safe, or slow down if you cannot
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires changing lanes away from stopped emergency and service vehicles, or slowing down if you cannot move over safely.

questions.prompt: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing on an undivided road, what must you do?
choices: Stop, regardless of your direction of travel
Continue if no children are visible
questions.explanation: On an undivided road, all drivers in both directions must stop for a school bus displaying flashing red lights.

questions.prompt: How much space should you give a motorcycle in a lane?
choices: A full lane, the same as any other vehicle
Half a lane so cars can share it
No space; they can squeeze in
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane and should be treated like any other vehicle.

questions.prompt: Why are large trucks dangerous to cut in front of?
choices: They need much longer distances to stop
They can stop faster than cars
questions.explanation: Trucks are heavy and need far more distance to stop, so cutting in front of one can cause a rear-end collision.

questions.prompt: When should you be especially alert for pedestrians?
choices: Near crosswalks, schools, and areas with children
questions.explanation: Pedestrians, especially children, are most common near crosswalks, schools, and neighborhoods, where extra caution is needed.

questions.prompt: When following a bicyclist, what should you do?
choices: Slow down and pass only with enough room
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have a right to the road, so slow down and give them plenty of space when passing.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid lingering alongside a large truck?
choices: You may be in the driver's blind spot
questions.explanation: The sides of a truck are major blind spots, so driving alongside one for long makes you hard for the driver to see.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you should do what?
choices: Yield and give them extra time and space
Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a pedestrian who may have a visual impairment, so you must yield and be patient.

questions.prompt: When approaching a stopped tow truck with amber lights flashing on the highway, what does the Move Over law require?
choices: Move over or slow down for it just like an emergency vehicle
Ignore it because it is not an emergency vehicle
questions.explanation: New Jersey's Move Over law covers tow trucks and highway maintenance vehicles, not just police and ambulances.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older in New Jersey?
questions.explanation: It is illegal for a driver 21 or older to operate a vehicle with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent or more.

questions.prompt: Under New Jersey's zero-tolerance law, what BAC makes it illegal for a driver under 21 to drive?
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face a zero-tolerance law, so a BAC of 0.01 percent or more is a violation.

questions.prompt: What is the only effective way to remove alcohol from your body?
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to eliminate alcohol; coffee, showers, and food do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: What does New Jersey law say about texting while driving?
choices: It is allowed at red lights
questions.explanation: New Jersey prohibits texting while driving, and violations carry escalating fines.

questions.prompt: Using a handheld cell phone while driving in New Jersey is what?
choices: Legal if you use one hand
questions.explanation: New Jersey bans the use of handheld phones while driving; hands-free use is allowed for adults but prohibited for GDL drivers.

questions.prompt: How can prescription or over-the-counter medications affect your driving?
choices: They can cause drowsiness and impair driving
They only matter for commercial drivers
questions.explanation: Many medications cause drowsiness or impairment, so read labels and ask a pharmacist before driving.

questions.prompt: What is the safest action if you feel drowsy while driving?
choices: Open a window and keep going
questions.explanation: The only real cure for drowsiness is to stop and rest; tricks like loud music do not restore alertness.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of distracted driving?
questions.explanation: Reading a text takes your eyes and mind off driving, making it a dangerous distraction.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It slows reactions and impairs judgment
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reaction time and impair the judgment needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to handle phone use before you start driving?
choices: Set navigation and put the phone away before driving
questions.explanation: Setting your route and putting the phone away before you drive keeps your attention on the road.

questions.prompt: In New Jersey, when must you turn on your headlights in relation to your windshield wipers?
choices: Whenever you use your wipers because of rain, snow, sleet, or fog
questions.explanation: New Jersey's wipers-on, headlights-on law requires headlights whenever wipers are needed due to weather.

questions.prompt: When is a road often most slippery during rain?
choices: During the first few minutes of rainfall
After it has rained all day
questions.explanation: Roads are most slippery when rain first mixes with oil and dust on the pavement.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to skid, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering gently where you want the front of the car to go helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: Which headlights should you use in heavy fog?
questions.explanation: Low beams cut through fog better, while high beams reflect off the fog and reduce your visibility.

questions.prompt: Which road surfaces freeze first in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from above and below, so they freeze before other parts of the road.

questions.prompt: When must you dim your high beams for an oncoming vehicle?
choices: Within 500 feet of the oncoming vehicle
questions.explanation: You must lower your high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle to avoid blinding the other driver.

questions.prompt: What should you do if conditions become too dangerous to drive during a severe storm?
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on your hazard lights
Speed up to get home faster
questions.explanation: If a storm makes driving unsafe, pull fully off the road, use hazard lights, and wait for conditions to improve.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your speed and following distance in bad weather?
choices: Slow down and increase following distance
Keep the same speed and distance
Speed up to reduce time on the road
Follow more closely to see better
questions.explanation: Rain, snow, and fog reduce traction and visibility, so slowing down and leaving more space is essential.

questions.prompt: When are you required to use headlights based on time of day?
choices: From a half hour after sunset to a half hour before sunrise
Only when it is fully dark
questions.explanation: Headlights are required from one half hour after sunset until one half hour before sunrise, and whenever visibility is poor.

questions.prompt: What is hydroplaning?
choices: Tires riding on a film of water and losing contact with the road
The engine stalling in deep water
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when tires ride up on water and lose grip; slowing down helps the tires keep contact with the road.

questions.prompt: How close to a fire hydrant does New Jersey law prohibit parking?
questions.explanation: New Jersey law prohibits parking within 10 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: Before leaving your parked vehicle, you should always do what?
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and shifting to park keeps the vehicle from rolling while it is unattended.

questions.prompt: When may you park in a space reserved for people with disabilities?
choices: Only with a valid disability permit or plate
When no other spaces are open
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces may only be used by vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or license plate.

questions.prompt: What should you check before pulling out of a parallel parking space?
choices: Mirrors, blind spots, and approaching traffic and bicyclists
questions.explanation: Checking mirrors, blind spots, and traffic, including bicyclists, before leaving a space prevents collisions.

questions.prompt: New Jersey law prohibits parking within how many feet of a crosswalk at an intersection?
questions.explanation: Parking within 25 feet of a crosswalk at an intersection is prohibited so drivers and pedestrians can see clearly.

questions.prompt: When parking on a public road, how should you position your vehicle?
choices: As close to the curb or edge as possible
In the middle of the lane
Far from the curb to be visible
questions.explanation: Parking close to the curb or road edge keeps your vehicle out of the path of moving traffic.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park?
choices: In front of a public or private driveway
In a marked parking lot space
On a residential street where parking is allowed
In a metered space with payment
questions.explanation: Blocking a driveway is illegal because it prevents vehicles from entering or leaving.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the car rolls back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: What must you do before reentering traffic from a parked position?
choices: Signal and yield to oncoming traffic
Pull out quickly so traffic stops
questions.explanation: You must signal and yield to traffic already on the road before moving out of a parking space.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a student learner's permit in New Jersey through driver education?
questions.explanation: A teen may apply for a student learner's permit at age 16 when enrolled in an approved driver education program.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must New Jersey permit holders under 21 complete (for permits issued on or after February 1, 2025)?
choices: At least 50 hours, including 10 at night
questions.explanation: Permit holders under 21 must log at least 50 hours of supervised driving, including 10 hours at night, before advancing.

questions.prompt: During which hours are New Jersey GDL permit and probationary drivers under 21 prohibited from driving?
questions.explanation: GDL drivers under 21 may not drive between 11:01 p.m. and 5:00 a.m. under New Jersey's curfew rule.

questions.prompt: What must drivers under 21 with a permit or probationary license display on their vehicle in New Jersey?
choices: Two red reflective decals on the front and rear plates
A learner's sign on the back window
questions.explanation: New Jersey requires under-21 GDL drivers to display two red reflective decals on the front and rear license plates.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the New Jersey written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The New Jersey knowledge test has 50 multiple-choice questions covering signs, laws, and safe driving.

questions.prompt: What percentage of questions must you answer correctly to pass the New Jersey knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the 50 questions correctly, meaning at least 40 right, to pass.

questions.prompt: Can a New Jersey GDL permit or probationary driver use a hands-free cell phone while driving?
choices: No, GDL drivers may not use any handheld or hands-free device
questions.explanation: GDL permit and probationary drivers are prohibited from using any electronic device, even hands-free, while driving.

questions.prompt: What document does a new driver under 21 receive after passing the road test, before a basic license?
questions.explanation: After passing the road test, an under-21 driver gets a probationary license that still carries GDL restrictions.

questions.prompt: What is required of New Jersey permit holders whenever they drive?
choices: A supervising licensed driver must be in the vehicle
They must drive only on highways
questions.explanation: Permit holders must always be supervised by a properly licensed adult driver while practicing.

questions.prompt: Of the 50 supervised driving hours required for under-21 permit holders, how many must be at night?
questions.explanation: The 50-hour supervised driving requirement includes at least 10 hours of nighttime driving practice.

questions.prompt: By driving on New Jersey roads, you give implied consent to what?
choices: Breath testing for alcohol when lawfully requested
Paying any fine without a hearing
Giving up your license on demand
questions.explanation: Driving means you have given implied consent to alcohol breath testing, and refusing carries its own penalties.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many motor vehicle points in New Jersey?
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points can lead to surcharges and suspension of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: New Jersey's seat belt law is enforced as a primary offense, which means what?
choices: An officer can stop you for a belt violation alone
You can only be ticketed after another violation
It applies only to the driver
questions.explanation: As a primary offense, a seat belt violation by itself is enough reason for an officer to stop and ticket you.

questions.prompt: At what age or height must a passenger wear a seat belt in New Jersey?
choices: At least 8 years old or 57 inches tall
questions.explanation: Passengers at least 8 years old or 57 inches tall must wear a seat belt; younger or smaller children need a child or booster seat.

questions.prompt: What is a likely consequence of a driving while intoxicated conviction in New Jersey?
choices: Fines, license suspension, and mandatory alcohol education
questions.explanation: A DWI conviction brings fines, license suspension, mandatory alcohol education, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: What happens if a driver refuses to take a breath test when lawfully requested?
questions.explanation: Refusing a lawful breath test is a violation that carries its own penalties under the implied consent law.

questions.prompt: Who must be properly secured in a child safety or booster seat in New Jersey?
choices: Children under 8 years old who are less than 57 inches tall
No one, if seat belts are worn
questions.explanation: Children under 8 and shorter than 57 inches must ride in an appropriate child safety or booster seat.

questions.prompt: What is a consequence for a GDL driver who violates the curfew, passenger, decal, or device rules?
choices: A fine for the GDL violation
questions.explanation: Breaking GDL restrictions such as the curfew, passenger, decal, or device rules results in a fine for the new driver.

questions.prompt: How can a driver reduce points on their New Jersey driving record over time?
choices: By maintaining a clean driving record
questions.explanation: Driving without violations over time allows points to be removed from your record.

questions.prompt: What may result from repeatedly texting while driving in New Jersey?
choices: Escalating fines and possible motor vehicle points
questions.explanation: Texting while driving carries escalating fines and, for repeat offenses, points and possible license suspension.
